patterns.title: SQL Injection
patterns.description: Unsanitized user input passed directly into a database query
examples: connection.execute('SELECT * FROM table WHERE x = ' + input)
patterns.remediation: Use parameterized queries or an ORM with safe defaults.

patterns.title: Insecure Direct Object Reference (IDOR)
patterns.description: Exposing internal object identifiers allows unauthorized access to other users' data
symptoms: using IDs directly from request params without auth checks
fetching records by ID without checking ownership
examples: const user = await db.users.findUnique({ where: { id: req.params.id } })
patterns.remediation: Always verify that the authenticated user has permission to access the requested resource.

patterns.title: Hardcoded Secrets
patterns.description: API keys, passwords, or secrets stored in plain text in source code
patterns.remediation: Move secrets to environment variables or a secure vault (Azure Key Vault, AWS Secrets Manager).

patterns.title: Unsanitized HTML Injection
patterns.description: Allowing untrusted input to be rendered as HTML in the browser
patterns.remediation: Sanitize user input using a library like DOMPurify or use secure alternatives like textContent.

patterns.title: Weak Password Hashing
patterns.description: Using obsolete or weak hashing algorithms for passwords
patterns.remediation: Use argon2 or bcrypt with a strong salt.
